title: La Estorie del Evangelie
summary: CME00079 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan encoding of section VI, pp. 545–610, of Gertrude H. Campbell's 1915 article 'The Middle English Evangelie.' Campbell prints two witness versions of the anonymous late-thirteenth-century Gospel poem conventionally called La Estorie del Evangelie: the 519-line opening fragment in Dulwich College MS 22 and the much longer version in Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Add. C. 38. Campbell worked from Carleton Brown's rotographs for the Dulwich text; her precise working medium for the Bodleian text is unstated. The XML serializes Campbell's parallel printed witness columns into two consecutive DIV2 streams and omits the article's linguistic/source study and final notes. Pons-Sanz's modern seven-witness census adds the Vernon Manuscript and four collateral witnesses: Rawl. C. 655, Senate House SLV/17, Royal MS 17 C XVII, and Lansdowne MS 388. A complete 710-canvas Vernon facsimile is cached and mapped to ff. 105r–105v; Rawl. C. 655 has only a cached one-opening public object whose target overlap is unproven; no complete public manuscript facsimiles were found for the other six witnesses. Whole-object request scopes and the best available scholarly text reproductions are recorded separately for every witness.
review_notes: The cataloged intellectual unit is one anonymous Middle English metrical Gospel poem represented by two separately serialized witness versions, not two independent works.

Campbell calls MS. Add. C. 38 a complete text, but current scholarship reconstructs a longer tradition from seven mutually non-derivative and individually incomplete witnesses. The Bodleian version is therefore described as Campbell's longest printed version rather than a complete authorial text.

Campbell's introductory Dulwich locus ends at fol. 84v, while her own transcription milestones reach fol. 85v and current scholarship locates the fragment at fols. 81v–85v. The modern range is retained provisionally and the discrepancy remains open.

The complete-source rule applies independently to each physical witness. Requests cover the whole surviving physical object and binding surfaces for Dulwich, Add. C. 38, Rawl. C. 655, SLV/17, Royal 17 C XVII, and Lansdowne 388, with work loci stated separately rather than used as crop boundaries.
The Vernon Manuscript is collateral evidence for the work title and tradition. It did not supply the two continuous texts encoded in CME00079.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00079
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00079
entities.description: A public keyed representation of Campbell's section VI: two witness-labeled verse streams, retained Latin rubrics, editorial notes, printed-page breaks, manuscript-folio milestones, and typography. It excludes the article's pp. 529–544 introduction and pp. 611–613 final notes.

entities.label: La Estorie del Evangelie
entities.description: An anonymous late-thirteenth-century Middle English metrical life of Christ based ultimately on the Gospels and probably mediated in part through Peter Comestor's Historia scholastica or a related compilation. Seven manuscript versions survive in fragmentary or revised form.

entities.label: Gertrude H. Campbell, The Middle English Evangelie (1915)
entities.description: The immediate printed source of CME00079. Campbell's article describes the manuscripts and textual relations, studies sources, dialect, and meter, and in section VI prints the Dulwich fragment and Bodleian version. CME encodes only section VI, pp. 545–610.

bibliographic.citation: Gertrude H. Campbell, 'The Middle English Evangelie,' Publications of the Modern Language Association of America 30, no. 3 (1915): 529–613, https://doi.org/10.1632/456948.
bibliographic.title: The Middle English Evangelie
bibliographic.container_title: Publications of the Modern Language Association of America

physical_description.extent: Article pp. 529–613; encoded section VI pp. 545–610
physical_description.format: Journal article with parallel single-witness diplomatic texts
notes: The Dulwich and Bodleian texts occupy parallel printed zones until the Dulwich fragment ends; the Bodleian version then continues alone.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of PMLA volume 30 (1915)
entities.description: A complete 1,042-page PDF of the bound journal volume containing Campbell's article on printed pp. 529–613. It is a print-volume facsimile, not a manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF scan of a bound journal volume

entities.label: Carleton Brown's rotographs of the Dulwich Evangelie fragment
entities.description: The photographic intermediary Carleton Brown placed at Campbell's disposal for editing the Dulwich text. Campbell does not identify the photographer, date, complete photographic extent, present repository, or survival status.

entities.label: London, Dulwich College, MS 22
entities.description: A composite vellum theological miscellany of 89 leaves in different thirteenth- and fourteenth-century hands. The Evangelie fragment is an inserted quire copied around 1300 and now localized to South Lincolnshire.

date_statements.label: Composite codex
date_statements.value: Thirteenth and fourteenth centuries, with some later additions

notes: The work is currently cited at fols. 81v–85v; Campbell's introduction and Warner's catalog give a shorter terminal locator, while Campbell's printed milestones reach fol. 85v.
The inserted quire originally belonged to another manuscript; its two outer leaves are lost. The first three surviving pages contain Latin theological material before the poem.
The poem is written in double columns, approximately 30–41 lines per column, with rubricated Latin passages and colored line initials.
Adjacent item 10 is a French Miracles of the Virgin fragment; it belongs to the complete physical object but not to this work.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Add. C. 38
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century English parchment codex containing part of the South English Legendary and La Estorie del Euangelie. Campbell's transcription places the Evangelie at fols. 71v–82r under a marginal Latin title.

physical_description.extent: i + 121 leaves according to the Summary Catalogue description quoted by Campbell
physical_description.format: Codex containing part of the South English Legendary and the Evangelie
notes: The work occupies fols. 71v–82r in current recto/verso notation; Campbell prints the legacy form 71b–82a.
Campbell describes one column with 40–46 written lines per page, normally placing both verses of an original couplet on one manuscript line.
The current catalog dates the codex 1400–1410; Campbell quotes the older 1410–1420 assessment.
The physical codex is described as imperfect at the end, which is distinct from whether its Evangelie version reaches a deliberate textual ending.

entities.label: Carl Horstmann, The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS, part 1 (1892)
entities.description: The prior printed edition of the Vernon fragment consulted by Campbell for textual comparison and line correspondence. It is not the source of Campbell's continuous Dulwich or Add. C. 38 transcriptions.

bibliographic.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS, part 1, Early English Text Society, Original Series 98 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1892), 1–11.
bibliographic.title: The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS, Part I
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS, part 1
entities.description: A complete 473-page PDF of Horstmann's EETS volume. The Evangelie fragment occupies printed pp. 1–11.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF scan of printed edition

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Eng. poet. a. 1
entities.description: A large, richly illuminated late-fourteenth-century West Midlands religious miscellany. Its 396-line Evangelie fragment occupies fols. 105r–105v and supplies the Anglo-Norman heading from which the conventional work title derives.

physical_description.extent: viii + 342 + i surviving leaves; originally viii + 413, with 71 leaves thought lost
notes: The Evangelie occupies fols. 105r–105v, Digital Bodleian canvases 193–194, and is incomplete after 396 lines.
Seven miniatures survive within this two-page fragment.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of the Vernon Manuscript
entities.description: The complete published 710-canvas color object, from upper cover through the final spine/lower-cover view. The Evangelie fragment is mapped within the whole codex to canvases 193–194.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 / 710 color JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: George F. Warner's 1881 Dulwich manuscript catalogue
entities.description: The complete historical catalog volume that describes MS 22, its ten contents, total 89-leaf extent, and Evangelie fragment.

bibliographic.citation: George F. Warner, Catalogue of the Manuscripts and Muniments of Alleyn's College of God's Gift at Dulwich, vol. 1 (London: Longmans, Green, 1881), 344–346.
bibliographic.title: Catalogue of the Manuscripts and Muniments of Alleyn's College of God's Gift at Dulwich

entities.label: Sara M. Pons-Sanz's 2021 study of La Estorie del Evangelie
entities.description: A peer-reviewed modern study that summarizes the seven-witness census, reconstructed date and region, witness loci, textual relations, and probable sources.

bibliographic.citation: Sara M. Pons-Sanz, 'Norse-derived vocabulary in La estorie del evangelie,' Folia Linguistica Historica 42, no. 2 (2021): 461–491, https://doi.org/10.1515/flin-2021-2032.
bibliographic.title: Norse-derived vocabulary in La estorie del evangelie

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS. Rawl. C. 655
entities.description: A fourteenth-century parchment Northern Passion manuscript in which 262 reconstructed lines of La Estorie del Evangelie are interpolated at Northern Passion lines 853–1057, 1388–1389, and 1727–1844. The Estorie folios have not yet been concorded to the codex images.

notes: The current TEI identifies parchment; Macray's older catalog said paper.
The Estorie survives as three interpolated runs in the host work, so host-work and target-work scopes must remain distinct.

entities.label: London, Senate House Library, MS SLV/17
entities.description: A manuscript dated 1403 whose present 114 leaves comprise 97 leaves of Piers Plowman followed by a 17-leaf La Estorie del Evangelie witness at ff. 97v–111v. Pons-Sanz reconstructs 1,764 Estorie lines and localizes its copy dialect to central Worcestershire.

notes: Piers Plowman occupies 97 leaves and La Estorie del Evangelie 17 leaves; the work locus is ff. 97v–111v.

entities.label: London, British Library, Royal MS 17 C XVII
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century manuscript containing a 501-line La Estorie del Evangelie fragment at ff. 152v–155v, corresponding to reconstructed lines 1905–2441. Pons-Sanz localizes its copy dialect to northern Lincolnshire.

notes: La Estorie del Evangelie occupies ff. 152v–155v.

entities.label: London, British Library, Lansdowne MS 388
entities.description: A fifteenth-century composite manuscript containing a 515-line La Estorie del Evangelie fragment at ff. 373r–380v, corresponding to reconstructed lines 1879–2404. Pons-Sanz localizes its copy dialect to the central East Midlands. The current item-level catalog begins an associated entry at f. 376, leaving a locus discrepancy to resolve.

physical_description.extent: Full extent not stated in the current parent record; the composite reaches at least f. 417
notes: Request the whole surviving physical object without inventing a terminal folio.
Pons-Sanz gives the Estorie locus as ff. 373r–380v; the current catalog's related item 20 begins at f. 376.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian one-opening object for MS. Rawl. C. 655
entities.description: A public one-canvas Digital Bodleian object reproducing ff. 17v–18r. It is a partial surrogate of the physical manuscript; overlap with the Estorie interpolations is not established.

entities.label: Bloomsbury Medieval Studies digitised primary-source item SHL003
entities.description: A subscription digital object described by the provider as reproducing the 97-leaf Piers Plowman portion of SLV/17. Coverage of the following 17-leaf Estorie witness and of the complete 114-leaf codex is unverified.

entities.label: Celia M. Millward, La Estorie del Evangelie: A Parallel-Text Edition (1998)
entities.description: The principal modern parallel-text scholarly edition of all seven witnesses and Pons-Sanz's stated primary source for reconstructed line numbering and the stemmatic account.

bibliographic.citation: Celia M. Millward, ed., La Estorie del Evangelie: A Parallel-Text Edition, Middle English Texts 30 (Heidelberg: Universitätsverlag Winter, 1998), 286 pp.
bibliographic.title: La Estorie del Evangelie: A Parallel-Text Edition

entities.label: Frances A. Foster, The Northern Passion, volume 2 (1916)
entities.description: A scholarly edition of the Northern Passion that transcribes and records variants from Rawl. C. 655, including its Estorie interpolations. It is a textual reproduction source, not a manuscript facsimile.

bibliographic.citation: Frances A. Foster, ed., The Northern Passion, volume 2, Early English Text Society, Original Series 147 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1916).
bibliographic.title: The Northern Passion

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Foster's Northern Passion, volume 2
entities.description: A complete 252-page scan of Foster's printed volume, retained as a scholarly transcription and variant source for Rawl. C. 655.

entities.label: R. H. Bowers, Three Middle English Religious Poems (1963)
entities.description: A scholarly transcription source for the Royal MS 17 C XVII Estorie fragment at ff. 152v–155v.

bibliographic.citation: R. H. Bowers, ed., Three Middle English Religious Poems (Gainesville: University of Florida Press, 1963), 65 pp.
bibliographic.title: Three Middle English Religious Poems

entities.label: HathiTrust Full View scan of Bowers's Three Middle English Religious Poems
entities.description: A public page-image representation of Bowers's printed edition. Automated retrieval was blocked, so no local copy is asserted.

entities.label: Folger Film Acc. 745, reel 8 surrogate lead for Lansdowne MS 388
entities.description: A microfilm-set entry whose public guide lists Lansdowne MS 388 on reel 8. The guide does not establish whether the reel reproduces the complete composite manuscript, the Estorie folios, or only Foxe-related portions.

notes: No complete public image object for MS 22 was found in the current Dulwich archive pages, the Henslowe-Alleyn digitization catalog, or exact-shelfmark searches. The Henslowe-Alleyn project's public images concern selected theatrical manuscripts and do not establish digitization of MS 22.
This dated negative finding does not prove that the manuscript has never been photographed or that internal images do not exist.
The archive welcomes enquiries and research appointments; in-depth research may incur a fee. Enquiries may be sent to archives@dulwich.org.uk.

Request a complete-object surrogate, not a work excerpt: front and rear covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all endleaves and blanks, every recto and verso of leaves 1–89, and edges when offered. State separately that the Evangelie is currently cited at fols. 81v–85v and ask the archivist to confirm the foliation conflict.
Ask for an item-specific quote, image specifications, delivery terms, and separate permission terms for repository redistribution or commercial publication.

notes: The current catalog TEI exposes no facsimile link, exact-shelfmark searches found no Digital Bodleian object, and direct tests of the intellectual-entity and catalog-record ARKs as Digital Bodleian object routes returned 404.
This is a dated public-access finding, not proof that no internal or older photography exists.

access.format: Reference-quality PDF scans supplied by file transfer
notes: The service is available to all, ordinarily charges non-Oxford requesters £1 per scan with a £10 minimum, and assesses conservation and legal suitability case by case. Completed forms and questions may be emailed to mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk.

For complete-source coverage request the entire physical object: covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, the front flyleaf, all rectos and versos of manuscript leaves 1–121 including blanks, and edges when offered. State separately that the Evangelie occupies fols. 71v–82r (Campbell 71b–82a).
Reference scans are not automatically authorized for repository redistribution or commercial print-on-demand use.

local_copies.label: Bodleian mediated copying order form

notes: The posted rate is £27 for the first image, £7.50 for images 2–99, and £6.15 for later images, with large orders negotiated.
Use the same complete-object surface list as for mediated copying and request a quote before assuming per-image pricing.
Separate permission is required for commercial and many non-academic uses.

editorial_practices.description: The encoding retains Campbell's section VI, pp. 545–610, while omitting the article's pp. 529–544 manuscript, relationship, source, dialect, and meter study and pp. 611–613 concluding notes.
Campbell's two printed witness columns are serialized as consecutive DIV2 elements. Repeated printed-page references describe overlapping zones of the same physical pages, not 98 distinct pages.

Campbell numbers both texts after D as far as D survives; the XML therefore contains a 1–519 common lineation and a much longer, discontinuous Bodleian sequence rather than independent continuous numbering for each witness.
Campbell restores the Bodleian manuscript's two-verses-per-line layout to couplet form in print.
Campbell states that punctuation and capitalization follow the manuscripts except for editorial hyphens.
Where a small guide letter survives in D, Campbell prints it in parentheses; where it is lacking, she supplies the initial in square brackets.

The XML retains 1,837 italic HI elements and four ABBR elements from the edition, including many expanded letters and Latin rubric typography. These tags describe the encoded edition layer, not an unmediated image of manuscript abbreviations.
editorial_practices.scope: Both witness streams and retained head matter

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, CME00079.xml at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.locator: IDG; TEXT/@LANG; BODY/DIV1/DIV2[1–2]; HEADNOTE, HEAD, L, LG, P, PB, MILESTONE, NOTE, HI, ABBR, and TRAILER
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for identifier, two witness-version divisions, titles, incipits and closing text, verse form, Middle English and embedded Latin, source page and manuscript folio milestones, apparatus, and encoded editorial practice.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME00079 structural audit
evidence.locator: whole XML tree and DIV2-scoped XPath counts
evidence.summary: Verifies one DIV1 and two DIV2 witness streams; 2,222 L elements in 133 LG groups; Dulwich with 519 lines, 69 LG, 35 descendant PB, 58 notes, and folio milestones 82r–85v; Bodleian with 1,703 line elements, 64 LG, 62 PB, 28 notes, and folio milestones 71v–82r; 98 total PB elements over printed pp. 545–610; and 1,837 italic spans.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection and browse inventory.
evidence.summary: Identifies the collection as publicly accessible, records The Middle English Evangelie edited by G. H. Campbell in PMLA 30 (1915), describes manual keying and proofreading from public-domain editions, and labels the collection No Copyright–United States.

evidence.citation: Gertrude H. Campbell, 'The Middle English Evangelie,' PMLA 30.3 (1915), 529–613.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the immediate edition, work title, two printed manuscripts and sigla, Carleton Brown rotographs, manuscript descriptions, textual relations, sources, date and dialect arguments, editorial statement, transcribed texts, page/folio mappings, and encoded selection boundary.

evidence.citation: Visual and text-layer inspection of the complete PMLA volume 30 PDF.
evidence.summary: Confirms article title and pagination, parallel-column witness layout, section VI headnote, printed page/folio markers, transition after the Dulwich fragment, Bodleian continuation, and final notes omitted from CME.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Core, 'The Middle English Evangelie,' PMLA 30.3 (1915), article record.
evidence.summary: Current publisher record for Campbell, article title, issue, 1915 publication, pp. 529–613, DOI, and abstract identifying the Dulwich and Bodleian texts.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for publications30modeuoft, PMLA volume 30.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete bound volume, University of Toronto source, 1,042-page object, direct PDF, 1915 date, and NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT status.

evidence.citation: George F. Warner, Catalogue of the Manuscripts and Muniments of Alleyn's College of God's Gift at Dulwich, vol. 1 (1881), 344–346.
evidence.summary: Historical institutional catalog evidence for shelfmark, composite contents, vellum support, 89-leaf extent, small-quarto format, inserted quire, adjacent items, circa-1300 dating, and the 519-line English Life of Christ fragment.

evidence.citation: Dulwich College, The Archive.
evidence.summary: Current holding-institution evidence that researchers are welcome by appointment, more involved research may incur a fee, and archive enquiries go to archives@dulwich.org.uk.

evidence.citation: Henslowe-Alleyn Digitisation Project, Copyrights, Reproductions, and Permissions.
evidence.summary: Directs requests for digital photographs or reproductions of Dulwich manuscripts and permission to reproduce them to the Keeper of the Archives. Its copyright statements concern the Henslowe-Alleyn project and are not generalized to an undelivered MS-22 image set.

evidence.citation: Dated search for a complete public Dulwich College MS 22 facsimile.
evidence.summary: Exact-shelfmark searches and review of the public Henslowe-Alleyn catalog found no complete public image object for MS 22; the digitization project's stated scope centers theatrical manuscripts.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Add. C. 38, manuscript_153.
evidence.locator: msIdentifier, msContents items 1–2, physDesc, and history/origin
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for exact shelfmark, ARKs, Summary Catalogue number, early-fifteenth-century date, English origin, parchment support, South English Legendary context, and La Estorie del Euangelie title.

evidence.citation: Dated Digital Bodleian and catalog-link check for MS. Add. C. 38.
evidence.summary: The catalog TEI exposes no facsimile link; exact-shelfmark searches found no Digital Bodleian object; and tested Digital Bodleian routes based on both catalog ARKs returned 404 on 2026-07-12.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying.
evidence.summary: Current request route for reference scans, available to all and subject to conservation, legal, and staffing review.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries mediated copying form.
evidence.summary: Records the £1-per-scan ordinary rate, £10 form minimum, delivery and payment process, request email, and non-commercial CC BY-NC statement.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Imaging Services: Order images.
evidence.summary: Current route for high-resolution color TIFF orders and pricing information.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services order form.
evidence.summary: Records the posted per-image color TIFF rates, required locators, delivery process, and CC BY-NC statement for out-of-copyright material.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Distinguishes permitted non-commercial and academic use from commercial reuse requiring permission and fees.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections requests.
evidence.summary: Official route and conditions for ordering special-collections material to a reading room.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Vernon Manuscript complete Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest.
evidence.locator: range 'La Estorie del Evangelie'; one-based Presentation positions 193–194
evidence.summary: Supplies the complete 710-canvas object, exact folio labels and range, catalog description, physical extent, date, place, binding, and image attribution under CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: Inventory, ZIP-integrity, and representative-image audit of the complete Vernon Manuscript IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: 710 images; one-based position 193 / fol. 105r / image 000193; one-based position 194 / fol. 105v / image 000194
evidence.summary: Confirms complete published-object coverage from upper cover to spine/lower cover, color access derivatives, the two-page Evangelie fragment and surviving miniatures, exact canvas URLs, and an error-free ZIP inventory.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS, part 1, EETS OS 98 (1892), 1–11.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for Horstmann's prior edition of the Vernon fragment, the Anglo-Norman heading, title history, text opening, and the edition Campbell consulted.

evidence.citation: Sara M. Pons-Sanz, 'Norse-derived vocabulary in La estorie del evangelie,' Folia Linguistica Historica 42.2 (2021): 461–491.
evidence.summary: Current peer-reviewed evidence for the seven-witness census, late-thirteenth-century composition, reconstructed Fenland region, Dulwich and Add. C. 38 loci and line counts, witness dates and dialects, lack of direct descent among surviving copies, textual revision, and probable Gospel/Historia scholastica source context.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Rawl. C. 655, manuscript_8298.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for shelfmark, fourteenth-century date, parchment support, 50-leaf extent, Northern Passion contents, and the Estorie interpolation line runs.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, one-opening IIIF object for MS. Rawl. C. 655.
evidence.summary: A complete cache of the one published full-resolution canvas. It is only a partial surrogate of the manuscript, and target-work overlap is unproven.

evidence.citation: William Dunn Macray, Catalogi codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecae Bodleianae, part V, fascicle 2, Rawlinson C quarto catalog.
evidence.summary: Complete historical catalog scan and Rawl. C. 655 entry. Its paper description is superseded by the current TEI's parchment identification.

evidence.citation: Frances A. Foster, ed., The Northern Passion, volume 2, EETS OS 147 (1916).
evidence.locator: Rawl. C. 655 text and variants; Estorie interpolations at Northern Passion lines 853–1057, 1388–1389, and 1727–1844
evidence.summary: Complete 252-page scan of the scholarly Northern Passion edition, providing a direct textual reproduction source for the Rawlinson witness and its Estorie interpolations.

evidence.citation: Senate House Library, SLV/17 archive record.
evidence.summary: Current official record for the Clopton manuscript, shelfmark SLV/17, date 1403, and composite contents.

evidence.citation: Senate House Library, Guide to the Medieval Manuscript Holdings (2022).
evidence.summary: Complete 56-page official guide supporting the present 114-leaf extent, date, and arrangement of the manuscript.

evidence.citation: Bloomsbury Medieval Studies, digitised primary-source item BMS_SHL003.
evidence.summary: Subscription item page describing a digitized 97-leaf Piers Plowman portion of Senate House SLV/17; it does not prove coverage of the following Estorie leaves or whole 114-leaf codex.

evidence.citation: Bloomsbury History, Terms and Conditions and Help.
evidence.summary: Current provider terms and help governing subscription access, personal research and private study, downloading, and restrictions on reproduction and commercial use.

evidence.citation: Senate House Library, Reproductions and Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Current official notice that reproduction and digitisation are unavailable until at least September 2026, with the last published special-collections prices and copyright or attribution conditions.

evidence.citation: Senate House Library, library and Virtual Reading Room information.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for the Special Collections Reading Room and a free remote Virtual Reading Room service open to members and external researchers by appointment.

evidence.citation: Universitätsverlag Winter, Celia M. Millward, La Estorie del Evangelie: A Parallel-Text Edition.
evidence.summary: Official publisher and library-catalog evidence for the 1998 seven-witness edition, 286-page extent, ISBN 978-3-8253-0750-9, current €61 purchase route, and library availability.

evidence.citation: British Library, Royal MS 17 C XVII.
evidence.summary: Current official catalog evidence for shelfmark, 1400–1440 date, 166-folio extent, and target locus.

evidence.citation: British Library, Lansdowne MS 388 parent record.
evidence.summary: Current official parent record for the composite manuscript and its itemized catalog structure; the total physical extent is not stated there.

evidence.citation: British Library, Lansdowne MS 388 item records 20, 21, and 24.
evidence.locator: related item entries beginning at ff. 376, 381, and 416
evidence.summary: Current item-level records bounding the relevant manuscript neighborhood and exposing the discrepancy between item 20's f. 376 start and Pons-Sanz's Estorie locus beginning f. 373r.

evidence.citation: Dated British Library catalog and public-image check for Royal MS 17 C XVII and Lansdowne MS 388.
evidence.summary: Neither current exact catalog record exposed a public complete image object, and exact-shelfmark searches found no replacement public facsimile on 2026-07-12. This is a dated negative finding, not proof that internal photography does not exist.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Current official order and consultation routes for British Library collection reproductions, subject to assessment and quotation.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions.
evidence.summary: Current official terms for supplied images, pricing, preservation constraints, permitted use, and order obligations.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, R. H. Bowers, Three Middle English Religious Poems (1963), record 84321.
evidence.summary: Official library record explicitly identifying the volume as a transcription source for the Royal Estorie fragment.

evidence.citation: HathiTrust, Three Middle English Religious Poems, record 001648972 and Full View item mdp.39076006161058.
evidence.summary: HathiTrust catalog marks the Michigan item Full View. The viewer was not retrievable by the automated client, so no local copy or unrestricted reuse license is asserted.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, Foxe and the English Reformation, c.1539–1587, Film Acc. 745, and Adam Matthew reel guide.
evidence.summary: The official Folger record and distributor guide establish a reel-8 entry for Lansdowne MS 388 but not its filmed extent or target-work coverage.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, Images and permissions.
evidence.summary: Current official image-request and permission route, including photo@folger.edu; it does not determine rights in the third-party Adam Matthew microfilm.

open_questions.question: What is the archive's current authoritative foliation for the Dulwich fragment: fols. 81v–85v or the shorter range reported in Campbell's introductory catalog quotation?
next_steps: Ask Dulwich College Archives to confirm the current work locus and whether foliation changed after Warner/Campbell.
Compare a complete supplied surrogate against Campbell's folio milestones and incipit/explicit.
Retain the provisional modern scholarly range rather than silently correcting the historical description.

open_questions.question: Did Campbell transcribe MS. Add. C. 38 directly from the codex, from photographs, or through another working copy?
next_steps: Search Campbell and Carleton Brown papers for correspondence, notes, or photographs.
Ask the Bodleian whether early-twentieth-century reproduction or reader records survive.
Do not invent a photographic intermediary for B without evidence.

open_questions.question: How should Campbell's description of B as a complete text be reconciled with modern scholarship that treats all seven versions as incomplete relative to the reconstructed tradition?
next_steps: Consult Millward's 1998 parallel-text edition directly, especially its reconstructed lineation and ending analysis.
Distinguish a coherent witness ending from completeness relative to the lost archetype.
Keep Campbell's and modern claims separately attributed.

open_questions.question: What compilation, if any, mediated between the Gospels, Peter Comestor and the Estorie author?
next_steps: Review Millward's source study and later biblical-literature scholarship.
Keep Historia scholastica as a probable related source, not an identified physical exemplar.
Separate sources for individual bestiary, homiletic, and apocryphal passages from the main narrative source.

open_questions.question: Can the holding institutions supply complete color surrogates of Dulwich MS 22, Bodleian MSS Add. C. 38 and Rawl. C. 655, Senate House SLV/17, Royal MS 17 C XVII, and Lansdowne MS 388 with terms permitting local study or broader reuse?
next_steps: Request full-object quotes using the distinct surface inventories recorded in each access record; do not reduce requests to the target-work folios.
Ask each repository to confirm existing photography or microfilm before commissioning new work.
Obtain written, item-specific redistribution and commercial-use terms before placing delivered images in Git or publication packages.
Recheck Senate House reproduction availability after September 2026.

open_questions.question: Can the late-thirteenth-century poem be localized more narrowly than the south Lincolnshire / north-west Norfolk / north-east Cambridgeshire Fenland zone?
next_steps: Review Millward 1998 and McIntosh 1987 directly.
Keep the copy dialects of D and B separate from the reconstructed original dialect.
Avoid converting dialect geography into a certain place of composition.

open_questions.question: Which Rawl. C. 655 folios contain each of the three Estorie interpolations, and does the public ff. 17v–18r opening overlap any of them?
next_steps: Concord Foster's Northern Passion lineation and the current catalog's interpolation runs to Rawl. C. 655 folios.
Do not label the one-canvas Digital Bodleian object as target-work coverage until the concordance proves overlap.
Request the complete codex rather than only the published opening.

open_questions.question: Does Bloomsbury item BMS_SHL003 include the 17-leaf Estorie portion of SLV/17, or only the provider-described 97-leaf Piers Plowman portion?
next_steps: Inspect the authenticated item inventory through a legitimate subscription or ask Bloomsbury and Senate House Library for exact coverage.
Do not treat the subscription object as an Estorie facsimile until ff. 97v–111v are confirmed.
Use the Virtual Reading Room to verify target folios while reproduction service is suspended.

open_questions.question: How should Pons-Sanz's Lansdowne Estorie locus ff. 373r–380v be reconciled with the current item record beginning at f. 376, and does Folger reel 8 cover those folios or the whole manuscript?
next_steps: Inspect the complete codex or an authoritative item-level description to resolve the f. 373r/f. 376 boundary.
Ask Folger or Adam Matthew for the reel-8 frame inventory before asserting target or whole-codex coverage.
Retain both claims as attributed evidence rather than silently choosing one.
